description: One of the oldest surviving imperial gardens in China, with the White Pagoda as its iconic structure.
A historic and cultural district in Beijing where traditional hutong charm blends with scenic lakes, offering enchanting night views.
The highest point on Beijing's central axis, providing the best panoramic view of the Forbidden City, and a former imperial garden from the Ming Dynasty.
The main venue area of the 2008 Olympics, home to iconic structures such as the Bird's Nest and Water Cube.
A top-tier science and engineering university in China, known for its beautiful campus and strong academic atmosphere.
The central square of Beijing, the capital of the People's Republic of China, and the largest city square in the world.
A fashion landmark in Beijing, featuring international brands and serving as a hub for modern urban nightlife.
A distinctive artistic hutong in Beijing, filled with creative shops and cafes, offering an experience of old Beijing hutong culture.
A renowned venue for traditional Chinese comedic performances (xiangsheng), housed in a classic theater building, providing an authentic xiangsheng cultural experience.
One of the earliest zoos opened in China, famous for housing rare animals such as giant pandas.
A quintessential example of ancient Chinese imperial gardens, a UNESCO World Heritage Site centered around Kunming Lake and Longevity Hill.
The ruins of a Qing Dynasty imperial garden, a historical witness to the 'Garden of Gardens' and a patriotic education base.
A commercial district with over 500 years of history, preserving the traditional hutong style and showcasing traditional commercial culture of old Beijing.
The Olympic swimming venue, featuring a unique membrane structure, now transformed into the Ice Cube Ice Sports Center.
An internationally renowned contemporary art district, a creative cultural park converted from former industrial factories.
The largest tomb in the Ming Dynasty imperial mausoleum complex, the Changling Tomb, which is the burial site of Emperor Yongle (Zhu Di).
A comprehensive exhibition of Beijing's history and culture, a modern museum with a rich collection of cultural relics.
